analysis_result: Approximately 47.6% of nuclei in the combined MPNST-G2 snRNA-seq cohort (M1677 + M1933) were classified as immune-lineage cells (7 Leiden clusters dominated by PTPRC/CD45 expression, including CD14/CD68/CD163+ macrophages/microglia, CD3D/CD8A+ T/NK cells). By-sample split: M1677 57.9% immune, M1933 36.5% immune. The simpler PTPRC-positive (log-norm expr > 0) proxy yielded 47.2%, a near-identical estimate.
denominator_used: N = 14,501 filtered MPNST-G2 nuclei (7,536 from M1677 + 6,965 from M1933) after QC: >=500 genes/nucleus and <=10% mitochondrial content. Denominator is the full post-QC MPNST-G2 snRNA-seq cell set.
assumptions_made: QC thresholds (min_genes=500, pct_mt<=10) were chosen as standard snRNA-seq defaults from the scanpy recipe; the paper's exact filtering thresholds were not visible to this analysis and may differ, which would shift the denominator and therefore the immune percentage; HVG selected with seurat_v3 flavor (n_top=3000), PCA n_comps=50, Leiden resolution=0.8 (random_state=42); immune clusters defined as Leiden clusters with >50% PTPRC+ fraction AND mean immune-signature score >0.1 (signature built from PTPRC, CD3D/E, CD8A, CD4, CD14, CD68, MS4A1, NKG7, CD19, CD163, CSF1R, ITGAM, TYROBP, FCER1G, AIF1, HLA-DRA); no batch integration between the two MPNST-G2 samples was performed; no doublet removal beyond the QC cutoffs; without a ground-truth CellTypist-like annotation the cluster-level immune call carries uncertainty for borderline clusters (e.g., cluster 4 had 26.5% PTPRC+ and a slightly negative immune score and was NOT called immune).
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
  - GSE207399_10x matrices for two MPNST-G2 samples: GSM6285488_M1677 and GSM6285489_M1933.
  - Each raw 10X matrix: 6,794,880 barcodes x 33,694 genes (full unfiltered 10X space).
  - PTPRC and all 17 probed immune markers (PTPRC, CD3D, CD3E, CD8A, CD4, CD14, CD68, MS4A1, NKG7, CD19, CD163, CSF1R, ITGAM, TYROBP, FCER1G, AIF1, HLA-DRA) present in both samples.

B. COHORT DEFINITION (filtering)
  - Dropped barcodes with <1 gene detected: M1677 6,794,880 -> 849,015; M1933 6,794,880 -> 1,170,565 (these are mostly empty droplets).
  - Applied QC: n_genes_by_counts >= 500 AND pct_counts_mt <= 10.
    * M1677: 849,015 -> 7,536
    * M1933: 1,170,565 -> 6,965
  - Final analytic set: 14,501 nuclei (7,536 + 6,965).

C. VARIABLE CODING
  - Normalization: normalize_total(target_sum=1e4) + log1p.
  - HVG: seurat_v3 on raw counts, n_top_genes=3,000.
  - Scaling on HVG subset (zero_center=True, max_value=10), PCA n_comps=50, neighbors n_neighbors=15, Leiden resolution=0.8 -> 16 clusters.
  - Immune signature: sc.tl.score_genes over the 17 markers above, stored as `immune_score` per nucleus.
  - Per-marker per-cluster mean log-normalized expression computed for PTPRC, CD3D, CD14, CD68, CD163, NKG7, TYROBP, AIF1, HLA-DRA (and others).

D. COMPUTATION
  - Immune cluster rule: ptprc_pos_frac > 0.5 AND immune_score_mean > 0.1.
  - Satisfied by Leiden clusters {0, 3, 6, 7, 8, 9, 14}. These have mean PTPRC log-norm expression 1.7-3.3 and include clear macrophage/microglial (CD14+/CD68+/CD163+/AIF1+/HLA-DRA+: 0, 3, 7) and T/NK-skewed (CD3D+/NKG7+: 6, 8, 9, 14) profiles.
  - Numerator (immune nuclei): 1,974 + 1,255 + 972 + 935 + 822 + 761 + 187 = 6,906.
  - Denominator (all post-QC MPNST-G2 nuclei): 14,501.
  - Proportion = 6,906 / 14,501 = 0.4762 (47.62%).
  - Cross-check proxy (PTPRC log-norm expr > 0): 6,851 / 14,501 = 47.24%.
  - Cross-check stringent (PTPRC log-norm expr > 1): 6,528 / 14,501 = 45.03%.

E. RESULT
  - Immune proportion in MPNST-G2 snRNA-seq: ~47.6% overall (cluster-based classification), 47.2% by PTPRC-positive proxy.
  - By sample: M1677 = 4,362 / 7,536 = 57.9%; M1933 = 2,544 / 6,965 = 36.5%.

F. CAVEATS
  - The paper's exact QC thresholds, clustering resolution, and immune-cluster assignment were not available to this (blinded) analysis. The percentage is sensitive to QC choices; more aggressive filtering (e.g., min_genes=1,000) reduces the denominator and can shift the immune percentage materially.
  - No batch/integration correction was applied between M1677 and M1933, but clustering was performed on the concatenated object; this can produce per-sample-enriched clusters and inflate intra-cluster homogeneity.
  - Cluster-based classification treats entire clusters as immune/non-immune even though some clusters are heterogeneous (cluster 12 = 42.6% PTPRC+, borderline; cluster 4 = 26.5% PTPRC+, not called immune). A stricter threshold would lower the immune percentage by a few points.
  - CellTypist-style reference annotation was not used, so cluster identity calls rely on marker enrichment heuristics.
  - The two samples had very different immune fractions (M1677 58% vs M1933 37%); the combined figure is an average weighted by post-QC nuclei count, not patients.
  - Agreement between the cluster method (47.6%) and the marker-only PTPRC+ proxy (47.2%) supports the estimate's robustness to the clustering step.
